analysis_result: WNT_UP.V1_UP in MPNST-G2 vs MPNST-G1: NES = 0.880, FDR q-value = 0.957 (not significant). Lead genes: CD74, NF1, VAMP1, CD96, RASGRP2, KLRC1, HS3ST1, AP1B1, CRMP1, DBP, etc. (35 of 127 genes). Also computed WNT_UP.V1_DN for reference: NES = 1.383, FDR = 0.272.
denominator_used: N = 12 MPNST RNA-seq samples: 6 MPNST-G1 (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M803) and 6 MPNST-G2 (M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413). Clinical annotation lists 7 MPNST-G2 but M1933 (RT1933) has no matching column in GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz, so it was excluded. 14,995 genes with non-zero variance entered the ranked list.
assumptions_made: GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz values already log-normalised (per GEO description and data instructions); used signal-to-noise ratio [(mean_G2 - mean_G1)/(sd_G2 + sd_G1)] to rank genes with MPNST-G2 as phenotype of interest (positive side); ran gseapy.prerank with the MSigDB C6 (oncogenic signatures) collection, version 2023.2.Hs, including WNT_UP.V1_UP and WNT_UP.V1_DN; 1000 permutations, seed=42, min_size=5, max_size=5000; sample M1933 excluded because no matching RNA-seq column in the expression matrix; FDR is the BH-adjusted permutation q-value reported by gseapy (FDR q-val column)
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
  - GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz: log-normalised bulk RNA-seq, 14,995 genes x 48 samples (gene symbols as rownames).
  - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx (Supplementary Data 1): clinical table with Methylation_Group, RNA-Seq flag and Tumor_ID (108 rows).

B. COHORT DEFINITION
  - Filter to RNA-Seq == 'X' -> 49 samples.
  - Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G1' -> 6 Tumor_IDs, all present in RNA matrix.
  - Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G2' -> 7 Tumor_IDs; 6 present in RNA matrix (M1933 / RT1933 absent, excluded).
  - Final analytic cohort: 12 samples (6 G2 + 6 G1).

C. VARIABLE CODING
  - Phenotype of interest (positive side): MPNST-G2.
  - Comparator (negative side): MPNST-G1.
  - Gene set: WNT_UP.V1_UP from MSigDB C6 oncogenic signatures (dbver 2023.2.Hs, pulled via gseapy.Msigdb.get_gmt).

D. COMPUTATION
  - Restricted matrix to 12 samples, dropped rows with NaN and zero variance -> 14,995 genes.
  - Computed signal-to-noise ranking metric per gene: s2n = (mean_G2 - mean_G1) / (sd_G2 + sd_G1 + 1e-6); higher values = upregulated in G2.
  - Wrote ranked list (gene, stat) to a .rnk file; wrote C6 gene sets to a .gmt file.
  - Ran gseapy.prerank(rnk, gene_sets=c6.gmt, permutation_num=1000, seed=42, min_size=5, max_size=5000, threads=4).
  - Extracted rows with 'WNT' in the term name.

E. RESULT
  - WNT_UP.V1_UP: ES = 0.2059, NES = 0.8801, NOM p-val = 0.712, FDR q-val = 0.9571, FWER = 1.0, leading-edge 35/127 (26.79%).
  - WNT_UP.V1_DN (for context): ES = 0.3214, NES = 1.3835, NOM p-val = 0.026, FDR q-val = 0.2718.
  - Cross-check using gseapy.gsea with phenotype permutation on the full expression matrix gave highly consistent values (WNT_UP.V1_UP NES ~= 0.83, FDR = 1.0), confirming the prerank result.

F. CAVEATS
  - Small sample size (6 vs 6) limits statistical power; FDR near 1 is consistent with no enrichment.
  - One MPNST-G2 sample (M1933) was not in the expression matrix and was excluded.
  - The paper's Supplementary Figure 6b reports fgsea-based results on a (likely) limma/voom moderated signed log10(p) ranking; the requested methodology here was gseapy prerank with a signal-to-noise ranking, so small numerical differences from the paper are expected.
  - FDR reflects permutation-based BH q-values across the full C6 collection (189 gene sets) as run in this analysis.
